commonIssues:
Many beneficiaries, especially senior citizens and manual laborers, cannot match their fingerprints (thumb impressions) on e-POS machines, resulting in denial of rations.

How to fix:

  • Under the state guidelines, shopkeepers must offer Aadhaar-OTP based authentication if fingerprints fail. Insist on receiving an OTP on your linked mobile number.
  • Alternatively, authorize a trusted nominee (via the RCMS portal) to collect rations on your behalf using their biometric data.

Between 2020 and 2025, over 2.49 crore ration cards were deleted or cancelled in Maharashtra due to eKYC mismatches, ineligibility, duplicate entries, or death, causing distress to many households.

How to fix:

  • Immediately check your status on the RCMS portal. If deleted wrongly, apply for reinstatement online or visit the local Tahsildar with a valid "Surviving Member Certificate" and recent income proof.
  • Always complete the annual e-KYC deadline specified by the state to avoid automated purges.

E-POS machines frequently encounter server issues and poor connectivity, especially in rural areas, leading to long waits and inability to receive grains.

How to fix:

  • There is an offline mode for e-POS machines that allows shopkeepers to distribute grains during network outages, which syncs once the network returns. Demand the shopkeeper to use this provision.
  • Avoid visiting shops on the 1st and 2nd of the month when server traffic is at its absolute peak.

In parts of Vidarbha and Marathwada, a pilot project replacing food grains with cash transfers has caused issues, with beneficiaries complaining of delayed payments and a lack of access to subsidized food.

How to fix:

  • Ensure your bank account is active, NPCI-mapped, and Aadhaar Seeded for Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT). If the transfer fails, it is usually because the account is frozen.
  • In areas where DBT is strictly enforced, you cannot opt back into physical grains; ensure local banking correspondents are utilizing microfilm devices if you cannot reach an ATM.

The state government cancelled over 18 lakh "bogus" (fake/ineligible) orange ration cards, causing confusion for those who were legitimate but had outdated income data, or those not having their Aadhaar linked.

How to fix:

  • Procure a freshly updated, accurate Income Certificate from Aaple Sarkar. Orange cards require income between ₹15,000 and ₹1 lakh.
  • Submit a formal petition to the local Tahsildar with the income proof and physical Aadhaar copies of all famiy members to legally challenge the "bogus" tag.

Complaints arise regarding shopkeepers overcharging, refusing to give receipts, or hoarding grains.

How to fix:

  • Beneficiaries in Maharashtra have "Portability" under the One Nation One Ration Card scheme. You are not forced to buy from a corrupt dealer—you can instantly switch to any other FPS in your city.
  • To punish misconduct, file a formal complaint using the toll-free helpline number 1800-22-4950 or 1967.

Many beneficiaries report not getting their full entitlement of grains.

How to fix:

  • Demand the printed receipt generated by the e-POS machine before leaving. This legally binds the seller to the weight dispensed.
  • If denied, you can claim a "Food Security Allowance" under Section 8 of the NFSA by formally writing to the District Grievance Redressal Officer (DGRO).
